baskets.label: Person harm
baskets.rationale: Person offences other than occupational assaults against police/emergency workers and dangerous/negligent acts such as dangerous driving.

baskets.label: Residential burglary
baskets.rationale: Residential aggravated and non-aggravated burglary only; commercial and unknown-location burglary are excluded.

baskets.label: Vehicle crime
baskets.rationale: Motor vehicle theft and theft from a motor vehicle.

baskets.label: Property damage
baskets.rationale: Criminal damage only; graffiti and other property damage categories are excluded.

excludedFromCandidateScore: Assaults of police, emergency services or other authorised officers

interpretation.direction: 10 is the lowest relative recorded-offence risk; 0 is the highest.
interpretation.scope: Candidate comparative Safety score for owner review only. It is not a probability of victimisation and is not connected to live Settle ranking.
limitations: The source measures offences recorded by Victoria Police, not all crime or personal experience of safety.
Suburb counts have no publisher-supplied suburb population rate.
Activity centres, hospitals, nightlife and transport hubs can attract offences committed by or against non-residents.
CSA replaces sensitive A10 and A30 counts of three or fewer with two when calculating totals.
Recorded data is extracted 18 days after the reference period and can move in later releases.
